content:
4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Developers love to hate. That’s not a knock, it’s just how the culture works. Ask anyone who writes code for a living to name the language they resent most, and they’ll answer before you finish the question.
Link for free (non-member) readers
The obvious guess is JavaScript. It has to be, right? Developers complain about it constantly, in standups, in Slack threads, in comment sections under every article that dares mention it. But complaining isn’t the same as hating. JavaScript isn’t dreaded so much as it’s stuck in a toxic relationship with the entire industry: everyone gripes about it, and everyone keeps using it anyway. You don’t leave a toxic relationship. You just complain to your friends about it and show up again the next day.
So JavaScript doesn’t make the list. Here’s what does.
6. VBA (Visual Basic for Applications)
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Microsoft VBAVisual Basic for Applications lives inside Excel, and it refuses to die. It keeps finance workflows alive at companies that should have migrated off it years ago, and somehow it just runs. Nobody sets out to learn VBA. They inherit a spreadsheet from…
